description:
You are an MLOps engineer who has just finished three training
    runs.  
    The training pipeline automatically produced two tab-separated
    metric files:

    1. /home/user/mlops/artifacts/experiments/alpha_metrics.tsv  
    2. /home/user/mlops/artifacts/experiments/beta_metrics.tsv  

    Each file has four tab-separated columns with an initial header
    row that looks exactly like this (using alpha_metrics.tsv as the
    example):

        epoch	run_id	alpha_acc	alpha_loss

    and three data rows, one per epoch.

    Your manager wants a **single, human-readable report** that
    places the epoch number in the first column, the accuracy of the
    Alpha run in the second column and the accuracy of the Beta run
    in the third column.

    1. Create the directory /home/user/mlops/reports if it does not
       already exist.
    2. Generate the report file
       /home/user/mlops/reports/accuracy_comparison.tsv using only
       standard command-line text-processing tools.  
       Hints:  
          • “cut” is perfect for slicing the correct accuracy
            columns out of the two existing files.  
          • “paste” can join the sliced columns side-by-side.  
          • You should keep the header row.
    3. The final file must meet **all** of the following format
       requirements so that our automated CI job can verify it:

       • File path: /home/user/mlops/reports/accuracy_comparison.tsv  
       • Tab-separated (ASCII TAB, i.e. 0x09)  
       • Exactly four lines: one header plus three data rows  
       • Exact header, in this order and spelling (case-sensitive):  
         epoch<TAB>alpha_acc<TAB>beta_acc  
       • The three data rows must contain the corresponding epoch
         number followed by the accuracy value for Alpha and the
         accuracy value for Beta taken from the original files,
         with **no extra columns**.  
       • Each line, including the last one, must end with a single
         Unix newline character (LF, 0x0A).

    Example of correct structure (values will be checked):

        epoch	alpha_acc	beta_acc
        1	    0.80	    0.78
        2	    0.82	    0.79
        3	    0.83	    0.81

    Do not hard-code the output; extract it programmatically
    from the source files.  
    You will probably need no more than three to four commands.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing files that the evaluation container will create
        before the agent starts.
    -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/mlops/artifacts/experiments/alpha_metrics.tsv"><![CDATA[
epoch	run_id	alpha_acc	alpha_loss
1	ALPHA-001	0.80	0.45
2	ALPHA-002	0.82	0.40
3	ALPHA-003	0.83	0.38
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/mlops/artifacts/experiments/beta_metrics.tsv"><![CDATA[
epoch	run_id	beta_acc	beta_loss
1	BETA-001	0.78	0.50
2	BETA-002	0.79	0.48
3	BETA-003	0.81	0.44
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <!--
        File that must exist when the agent finishes.
    -->
    <expected_output>
        <file path="/home/user/mlops/reports/accuracy_comparison.tsv"><![CDATA[
epoch	alpha_acc	beta_acc
1	0.80	0.78
2	0.82	0.79
3	0.83	0.81
]]></file>
    </expected_output>
